name: Default Parameter Set
description: Boolean flag indicating whether given set of planet parameters has been selected as default (1=yes 0=no)

name: Solution Type
description: Disposition of planet according to given planet parameter set

name: Controversial Flag
description: Flag indicating whether the confirmation status of a planet has been questioned in the published literature (1=yes 0=no)

name: Planet Name
description: Planet name most commonly used in the literature

name: Host Name
description: Stellar name most commonly used in the literature

name: Number of Stars
description: Number of gravitationally bound stars in the planetary system

name: Number of Planets
description: Number of confirmed planets in the planetary system

name: Number of Moons
description: Number of moons in the planetary system

name: Circumbinary Flag
description: Flag indicating whether the planet orbits a binary system (1=yes 0=no)

name: Discovery Method
description: Method by which the planet was first identified

name: Discovery Year
description: Year the planet was discovered

name: Discovery Locale
description: Location of observation of planet discovery (Ground or Space)

name: Detections by Radial Velocity Variations
description: Flag indicating if the planet host star exhibits radial velocity variations due to the planet (1=yes 0=no)

name: Detected by Pulsar Timing Variations
description: Boolean flag indicating if the planet host star exhibits pulsar timing variations due to the planet (1=yes 0=no)

name: Detected by Pulsation Timing Variations
description: Boolean flag indicating if the planet host star exhibits pulsation timing variations due to the planet (1=yes 0=no)

name: Detected by Transits
description: Flag indicating if the planet transits its host star (1=yes 0=no)

name: Detected by Astrometric Variations
description: Flag indicating if the planet host star exhibits astrometric variations due to the planet (1=yes 0=no)

name: Detected by Orbital Brightness Modulations
description: Flag indicating whether the planet exhibits orbital modulations on the phase curve (1=yes 0=no)

name: Detected by Microlensing
description: Boolean flag indicating if the planetary system acted as a lens during an observed microlensing event (1=yes 0=no)

name: Detected by Eclipse Timing Variations
description: Flag indicating whether a circumbinary planet induces eclipse timing variations (1=yes 0=no)

name: Detected by Imaging
description: Flag indicating if the planet has been observed via imaging techniques (1=yes 0=no)

name: Detection by Disk Kinematics
description: Boolean flag indicating if the presence of the planet was inferred due to disk kinematics (1=yes 0=no)

name: Planetary Parameter Reference
description: Reference of publication used for given planet parameter set

name: Orbital Period [days]
description: Time the planet takes to make a complete orbit around the host star or system

name: Orbit Semi-Major Axis [au]
description: The longest radius of an elliptic orbit

name: Planet Radius [Earth Radius]
description: Length from center of planet to surface in Earth radii

name: Planet Radius [Jupiter Radius]
description: Length from center of planet to surface in Jupiter radii

name: Planet Mass [Earth Mass]
description: Amount of matter contained in the planet in Earth masses

name: Planet Mass [Jupiter Mass]
description: Amount of matter contained in the planet in Jupiter masses

name: Planet Mass or Mass*sin(i) [Earth Mass]
description: Best planet mass estimate available in Earth masses

name: Planet Mass or Mass*sin(i) [Jupiter Mass]
description: Best planet mass estimate available in Jupiter masses

name: Planet Mass Provenance
description: Provenance of the measurement of the best mass

name: Planet Density [g/cm^3]
description: Amount of mass per unit volume of the planet

name: Eccentricity
description: Amount by which the orbit deviates from a perfect circle

name: Insolation Flux [Earth Flux]
description: Insolation flux relative to Earth

name: Equilibrium Temperature [K]
description: Equilibrium temperature of the planet

name: Inclination [deg]
description: Angle of orbital plane relative to line-of-sight

name: Time of Conjunction (Transit Midpoint)
description: Midpoint of transit event

name: Time Reference Frame
description: Time system basis for temporal and orbital parameters

name: Transit Timing Variations Flag
description: Flag indicating if orbit exhibits transit timing variations

name: Impact Parameter
description: Projected distance between star and planet centers

name: Transit Depth [%]
description: Relative flux decrease during transit

name: Transit Duration [hrs]
description: Time from start to end of transit

name: Semi-Major Axis to Stellar Radius Ratio
description: Distance divided by stellar radius

name: Planet to Stellar Radius Ratio
description: Planet radius divided by stellar radius

name: Occultation Depth [%]
description: Depth of secondary eclipse

name: Epoch of Periastron
description: Time of closest approach

name: Argument of Periastron
description: Angular separation between node and periastron

name: Radial Velocity Amplitude
description: Half peak-to-peak radial velocity variation

name: Projected Obliquity
description: Angle between stellar rotation and orbital axis (projected)

name: True Obliquity
description: Angle between stellar rotation and orbital axis

name: Stellar Parameter Reference
description: Reference of publication for stellar parameters

name: Spectral Type
description: Classification based on spectral characteristics

name: Stellar Effective Temperature [K]
description: Temperature of the star

name: Stellar Radius [Solar Radius]
description: Radius of the star in solar units

name: Stellar Mass [Solar mass]
description: Mass of the star in solar units

name: Stellar Metallicity [dex]
description: Metal content relative to hydrogen

name: Stellar Metallicity Ratio
description: Ratio for metallicity value

name: Stellar Luminosity
description: Energy output per unit time

name: Stellar Surface Gravity
description: Gravitational acceleration at surface

name: Stellar Age [Gyr]
description: Age of the star

name: Stellar Density
description: Mass per unit volume of star

name: Stellar Rotational Velocity
description: Rotational velocity times sin(i)

name: Stellar Rotation Period
description: Time for one rotation

name: Systemic Radial Velocity
description: Velocity along line-of-sight

name: System Parameter Reference
description: Reference of publication for system parameters

name: Total Proper Motion
description: Angular change in position over time

name: Proper Motion (RA)
description: Angular change in right ascension

name: Proper Motion (Dec)
description: Angular change in declination

name: Distance [pc]
description: Distance to system in parsecs

name: Parallax
description: Angular position shift due to Earth orbit

name: RA [sexagesimal]
description: Right ascension in sexagesimal format

name: Dec [sexagesimal]
description: Declination in sexagesimal format

name: RA [decimal]
description: Right ascension in decimal degrees

name: Dec [decimal]
description: Declination in decimal degrees

name: Galactic Latitude
description: Galactic latitude in degrees

name: Galactic Longitude
description: Galactic longitude in degrees

name: Ecliptic Latitude
description: Ecliptic latitude in degrees

name: Ecliptic Longitude
description: Ecliptic longitude in degrees

name: B Magnitude
description: Brightness in B band

name: V Magnitude
description: Brightness in V band

name: J Magnitude
description: Brightness in J band

name: H Magnitude
description: Brightness in H band

name: K Magnitude
description: Brightness in K band

name: u Magnitude
description: Brightness in u band

name: g Magnitude
description: Brightness in g band

name: r Magnitude
description: Brightness in r band

name: i Magnitude
description: Brightness in i band

name: z Magnitude
description: Brightness in z band

name: W1 Magnitude
description: Brightness in W1 band

name: W2 Magnitude
description: Brightness in W2 band

name: W3 Magnitude
description: Brightness in W3 band

name: W4 Magnitude
description: Brightness in W4 band

name: Gaia Magnitude
description: Brightness in Gaia band

name: I Magnitude
description: Brightness in I band

name: TESS Magnitude
description: Brightness in TESS band

name: Kepler Magnitude
description: Brightness in Kepler band
